title: [MSFT] Design partition support
body:
The "design partition" feature supports scenarios wherein the designer needs the RTL output module hierarchy to be different than the logical hierarchy they are designing. With this feature, they just create a design partition then "tag" various entities with it. Upon compilation, CIRCT will refactor the hierarchy to move the tagged entities into the specified target partition. The design partition _must_ be found in the module hierarchy above (or in the same module as) the tagged entities.

- [x] Modeling in CIRCT and PyCDE
- [x] Move entities in the same module as the partition
- [x] Partially sort the modules by their uses. (So we only have to run one "bubbling" pass.)
- [x] Bubble up entities which aren't in the same module as their target partition.
- [x] If multiple entities are connected, float/bury the connection.
- [x] Bubble up any "wire manipulation" ops (eg array_*)
- [x] Update the placement paths
- [x] Expose through PyCDE

Minor "cleanup" tasks:
- [x] Where one value is passed in twice, merge.
- [ ] Copy wire manipulation ops which are used by ops both in and out of a design partition.
